step_by_step_thinking:
Clinical Reasoning:
Tumor-specific antigens are derived from proteins encoded by genes. The listed examples include CEA, HER2, EGFR, CA125, GD2, etc., all of which originate from known protein-coding genes. These antigens are recognized by immune systems through their corresponding gene products. Even when mutated or aberrantly expressed, these molecules still stem from original protein-coding sequences.
